agent_output: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Implement a pure TypeScript function `recalculatePrice(quantity: number, basePrice: number): number` that computes the final price after applying bulk discounts. This function will be called inline from a high-traffic pricing endpoint (~500 req/sec peak). No external dependencies, no I/O, no state — just a deterministic calculation with a unit test suite.","domain_rules":[{"rule":"Final price = base price minus applicable discounts, floored at $0.01. Price can never be zero or negative.","testable_assertion":"For any valid inputs, recalculatePrice always returns a value >= 0.01"},{"rule":"Bulk discount of 10% applies when quantity >= 10.","testable_assertion":"recalculatePrice(10, X) === X * 0.9 for any base price X (subject to floor); recalculatePrice(9, X) === X"}],"architecture_guidance":[{"decision":"Pure TypeScript function, no external dependencies","implementation_note":"Implement as `recalculatePrice(quantity: number, basePrice: number): number`. Stateless, deterministic, no I/O. Suitable for inline execution at 500 req/sec."},{"decision":"Unit tests only — no integration tests needed","implementation_note":"Cover all acceptance criteria plus edge cases (quantity boundary at 9/10, floor behavior, very small base prices). Pure function makes this straightforward."},{"decision":"Add JSDoc documenting the $0.01 floor behavior","implementation_note":"On-call engineers need to understand why a price might return $0.01 instead of $0.00. Document the floor rule and the business rationale directly above the function."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"Bulk discount applied at quantity threshold","given":"quantity=10, basePrice=$5.00","when":"recalculatePrice is called","then":"returns 4.50"},{"criterion":"No discount below quantity threshold","given":"quantity=9, basePrice=$5.00","when":"recalculatePrice is called","then":"returns 5.00"},{"criterion":"Price floor prevents zero or negative price","given":"a discount that would reduce price to $0.00 or below","when":"recalculatePrice is called","then":"returns 0.01"}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"Bulk discount","technical_term":"10% multiplier when quantity >= 10","definition":"A 10% reduction applied to basePrice when the quantity parameter meets or exceeds 10 units"},{"domain_term":"Price floor","technical_term":"Math.max(result, 0.01)","definition":"Business constraint that the returned price must never be less than $0.01, enforced after all discounts are applied"}],"contradictions_resolved":[],"open_questions":[]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":97,"high_confidence_areas":["All three agents agree on function signature, purity, and statelessness","Domain rules and acceptance criteria are consistent with each other — no contradictions","Operational constraints (inline, no I/O) align perfectly with the pure-function architecture"],"low_confidence_areas":["Floating-point precision handling is not explicitly addressed — Shaq should consider rounding to 2 decimal places for currency"],"assumptions":["basePrice is always a positive number (no validation rules were specified for negative basePrice inputs)","The 10% discount is the only discount tier — no mention of other thresholds","Return type is a plain number representing dollars, not a Money/Decimal type"]}}
